indications.peds_dose: 15 mg/kg (Max: 650 mg)
contraindications: Unable to swallow or maintain airway
Active vomiting
Took acetaminophen or combination product (e.g., Norco, Percocet, Vicodin, cold medicine) in last 6 hours
Severe jaundice or signs of liver failure
indications.when_to_give: Administer to all acutely painful conditions as the first-line oral analgesic. Given at the start of pain management before escalation to opioids is considered.

indications.adult_dose: 12 mg rapid IV push; may repeat ONCE at 12 mg
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mg/kg rapid IV push (Max: 6 mg); may repeat ONCE at 0.2 mg/kg (Max: 12 mg)
indications.route: IV (rapid push — must be given as fast bolus followed by saline flush)
indications.when_to_give: For hemodynamically stable supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) that fails vagal maneuvers. The ECG must show a narrow-complex, regular rhythm consistent with SVT before adenosine is used.

indications.adult_dose: 2.5 mg combined with Ipratropium 0.5 mg, Nebulized; may repeat twice as needed
indications.peds_dose: 2.5 mg combined with Ipratropium 0.5 mg, Nebulized; may repeat twice as needed
indications.when_to_give: Standing-order treatment for allergic reactions **without** anaphylactic features (no hypotension, no severe signs such as stridor/hypoxia/airway swelling, and symptoms not spanning ≥2 body systems), when wheezing or dyspnea is present. Given alongside Diphenhydramine for itching/rash. Not used for anaphylaxis — those patients go to the epinephrine branch.

indications.adult_dose: 10 mg Nebulized (equivalent to four 2.5 mg treatments)
indications.peds_dose: 10 mg Nebulized
contraindications: Significant tachycardia
Likely ischemic chest pain
indications.when_to_give: Given in conjunction with calcium chloride for ECG changes suggesting hyperkalemia. Albuterol drives potassium into cells as a temporizing measure.

indications.adult_dose: 5 mg Nebulized (with or without Ipratropium), every 10 min, up to 3 doses
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Pediatric)
indications.when_to_give: For mild-to-moderate asthma/COPD exacerbation with wheezing. Repeated every 10 minutes as needed for up to three total doses.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 5 mg Nebulized (with or without Ipratropium), every 10 min, up to 3 doses
indications.when_to_give: For mild-to-moderate pediatric asthma or reactive airway disease with wheezing. Not first-line for children <2 y/o whose wheezing is more likely bronchiolitis.

indications.adult_dose: 5 mg Nebulized (with or without Ipratropium)
indications.peds_dose: 5 mg Nebulized (with or without Ipratropium)
indications.when_to_give: Consider for bronchospasm from respiratory irritant chemical exposure (e.g., ammonia, chlorine, phosgene).

indications.adult_dose: 300 mg IV/IO after 2nd shock; then 150 mg (half dose) after 3rd shock
indications.peds_dose: 5 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 300 mg) after 2nd shock; then 2.5 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 150 mg) after 3rd shock
contraindications: Do NOT administer if atrial fibrillation with Wolff-Parkinson-White (WPW) syndrome is suspected (irregular, very high-rate wide-complex tachycardia with variable QRS morphology)
indications.when_to_give: For persistent ventricular fibrillation (VF) or pulseless ventricular tachycardia (pVT) after the second defibrillation shock has failed. Given during ongoing resuscitation.

indications.adult_dose: 150 mg IV/IO over 10 min; may repeat ONCE
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Tachycardia – Unstable (Pediatric)
contraindications: Do NOT administer if WPW-related wide-complex tachycardia is suspected
indications.when_to_give: For hemodynamically unstable wide-complex tachycardia with confirmed pulse and regular rhythm consistent with ventricular tachycardia (VT). Given alongside or after synchronized cardioversion attempts.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Tachycardia – Unstable (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 5 mg/kg IV/IO over 10 min (Max: 150 mg); may repeat ONCE
contraindications: Do NOT administer if WPW-related wide-complex tachycardia is suspected
indications.when_to_give: For hemodynamically unstable pediatric wide-complex tachycardia with pulse consistent with VT. Given alongside or after synchronized cardioversion attempts.

indications.adult_dose: 324 mg (chew and swallow)
indications.peds_dose: Not specified (Chest Pain CPG is adult-focused: ≥14 y/o)
indications.when_to_give: Given to all chest pain patients as initial treatment. Administered early, before other agents, as soon as ischemic cardiac etiology is possible.

indications.adult_dose: 1 mg IV/IO; may repeat every 3–5 min (Max total: 3 mg)
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Bradycardia (Pediatric)
contraindications: May not be effective for 2nd- or 3rd-degree AV heart blocks
indications.when_to_give: Given to hemodynamically unstable adult bradycardia patients while preparing for transcutaneous pacing. Repeated doses may be given while pacing is being set up.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Bradycardia (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO (Max single dose: 0.5 mg); may repeat ONCE if effective
indications.when_to_give: For hemodynamically significant pediatric bradycardia that does not respond to Epinephrine, or as an adjunct. May be repeated one time if an initial dose shows effect.

indications.indication: Toxic Chemical Exposure (Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Toxicity)
indications.adult_dose: 2 mg IV/IO/IM every 3–5 min until DUMBBELS signs/secretions improve (up to 3 total doses if using autoinjector; repeat as needed if using standalone Atropine)
indications.peds_dose: 0.05 mg/kg IV/IO/IM per dose (Max single dose: 2 mg) every 3–5 min until DUMBBELS symptoms improve
indications.route: IV/IO/IM (also available as combined Pralidoxime/Atropine autoinjector if stocked)
indications.when_to_give: Given for cholinergic toxidrome (DUMBBELS) from nerve agent or organophosphate pesticide exposure. Titrated to reduction in secretions and bronchospasm, not to heart rate. Pralidoxime/Atropine autoinjector preferred if available.

indications.adult_dose: 1 g IV/IO
indications.peds_dose: 20 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 1 g)
indications.when_to_give: Considered as a reversible cause treatment for hyperkalemia-related cardiac arrest (one of the "H's and T's"). Given during resuscitation when hyperkalemia is suspected.

indications.adult_dose: 1 g IV/IO over 5–10 min
indications.peds_dose: 20 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 1 g)
indications.when_to_give: For suspected calcium channel blocker overdose presenting with hemodynamic instability. Administered slowly to counteract the calcium-blocking effects of the drug.

indications.adult_dose: 1 g slow IV/IO over 5–10 min; may give half dose if ECG not improved
indications.peds_dose: 20 mg/kg slow IV/IO (Max: 1 g); may give half dose if ECG not improved
indications.when_to_give: For ECG changes consistent with hyperkalemia (peaked T waves, widened QRS, sine wave pattern). First-line agent to stabilize the cardiac membrane before potassium-lowering measures.

indications.adult_dose: 10 mg IV/IM/PO
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Pediatric)
indications.when_to_give: For moderate-to-severe adult asthma/COPD exacerbation, given alongside nebulized bronchodilators. Reduces airway inflammation.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 0.5 mg/kg IV/IM (Max: 10 mg)
indications.when_to_give: For moderate-to-severe pediatric asthma or reactive airway disease, given alongside nebulized bronchodilators to reduce airway inflammation.

indications.adult_dose: 125 mL IV/IO over 10 min; may repeat same dose if POC glucose not improved at 10 min
indications.peds_dose: 4 mL/kg IV/IO (Max: 125 mL); may repeat same dose if POC glucose not improved at 10 min
indications.when_to_give: For hypoglycemia with altered mental status or inability to protect the airway (i.e., patient cannot take oral glucose). First-line IV treatment for low blood sugar.

indications.adult_dose: N/A
indications.peds_dose: 2 mL/kg IV/IO
indications.when_to_give: For a newborn requiring resuscitation who also has a POC glucose <50 g/dL. Addresses neonatal hypoglycemia concurrent with resuscitative efforts.

indications.adult_dose: 25 mg IV/IM
indications.peds_dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IM (Max: 25 mg)
indications.when_to_give: For itching, rash, hives, or swelling in a non-anaphylactic allergic reaction (no hypotension, no severe signs such as stridor/hypoxia/airway swelling, and symptoms not spanning ≥2 body systems). Not given on the anaphylaxis branch.

indications.adult_dose: 50 mg IV/IO/IM
indications.peds_dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (Max: 50 mg)
indications.when_to_give: For acute dystonic reaction or torticollis caused by antipsychotic medications. Diphenhydramine is the antidote for extrapyramidal side effects.

indications.adult_dose: 0.3 mg IM if ≥25 kg; 0.15 mg IM if <25 kg; anterolateral thigh; may repeat ONCE at same dose if no improvement at 5 min
indications.peds_dose: 0.15 mg IM if <25 kg; 0.3 mg IM if ≥25 kg; anterolateral thigh; may repeat ONCE at same dose if no improvement at 5 min
indications.when_to_give: First-line and highest priority treatment for anaphylaxis. Given immediately upon recognition of anaphylactic reaction (hypotension, airway compromise, or systemic allergic symptoms after exposure).

indications.indication: Bradycardia – Adult (Pacing Failure) — Push Dose Epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL → diluted to 10 mcg/mL)
indications.adult_dose: Per Push Dose Epinephrine Procedure: 20 mcg (2 mL of 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO every 2–5 min
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Bradycardia (Pediatric)
contraindications: Cardiogenic shock from inferior STEMI (may worsen)
indications.when_to_give: If transcutaneous pacing fails in adult bradycardia, or as a bridge while pacing is being established. Used to maintain perfusion pressure. Also available as an epinephrine infusion: 2–10 mcg/min IV/IO.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Bradycardia (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 0.01 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 1 mg); 0.1 mg/mL formulation; repeat every 3–5 min if bradycardia persists
indications.when_to_give: First-line agent for hemodynamically significant pediatric bradycardia. Given repeatedly every 3–5 min while the underlying cause is treated.

indications.adult_dose: 1 mg IV/IO every 3–5 min (up to 3 doses)
indications.peds_dose: 0.01 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 1 mg) every 3–5 min (up to 3 doses)
indications.when_to_give: Given throughout resuscitation for all cardiac arrest rhythms. Administered every 3–5 min to maintain coronary perfusion pressure during CPR.

indications.indication: Cardiac Arrest – Post-ROSC Care — Push Dose / Infusion
indications.adult_dose: Push Dose Epinephrine per procedure (20 mcg IV/IO push), as alternative to or bridge before norepinephrine infusion
indications.peds_dose: Push Dose Epinephrine per procedure (1 mcg/kg, Max 20 mcg, IV/IO push)
contraindications: Cardiogenic shock from inferior STEMI
indications.when_to_give: If SBP <90 mmHg after ROSC and while preparing norepinephrine infusion, or if norepinephrine is unavailable. Provides rapid pressor support.

indications.adult_dose: N/A
indications.peds_dose: 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO with 5 mL flush; repeat every 3–5 min for persistent HR <60
indications.when_to_give: For a newborn with persistent bradycardia (HR <60) after adequate ventilation and chest compressions have been established.

indications.indication: Push Dose Epinephrine Procedure — Epinephrine (diluted to 10 mcg/mL)
indications.adult_dose: 20 mcg (2 mL of 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO push every 2–5 min; flush with 2–3 mL NS after each dose
indications.peds_dose: 1 mcg/kg (10 mcg/mL) IV/IO push (Max: 20 mcg) every 2–5 min; flush with 1–2 mL NS after each dose
contraindications: Cardiogenic shock from **inferior STEMI** (ST elevation in II, III, aVF) — may worsen
Use isotonic fluid boluses per Chest Pain CPG instead
indications.when_to_give: For shock or hypotension not responding to fluid resuscitation or other first-line treatments, across any protocol. Preparation required: dilute to 10 mcg/mL before use (three preparation methods provided in protocol).

indications.indication: Respiratory Distress – Adult (Respiratory Failure) — Epinephrine (1 mg/mL)
indications.adult_dose: 0.3 mg IM
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Pediatric)
indications.when_to_give: For adult asthma/COPD patients in respiratory failure (i.e., maximal bronchodilator therapy has failed and the patient is deteriorating). Last pharmacologic escalation before intubation.

indications.indication: Respiratory Distress – Pediatric (Respiratory Failure) — Epinephrine (1 mg/mL)
indications.adult_dose: N/A
indications.peds_dose: 0.01 mg/kg IM (Max: 0.3 mg); **OR 5 mg Nebulized** for croup/epiglottitis
indications.when_to_give: IM: for pediatric respiratory failure from severe asthma after bronchodilators fail. Nebulized: for stridor with signs of croup or epiglottitis (stridor, drooling, gagging).

indications.adult_dose: 50 mcg IV/IO/IN; may repeat if inadequate relief after 5–10 min
indications.peds_dose: Not listed in Chest Pain CPG (adult protocol)
contraindications: SBP <100 mmHg
Given only after nitroglycerin and if continued pain persists
indications.when_to_give: For continued chest pain after nitroglycerin in patients with SBP >100 mmHg. Not used as first-line; reserved for pain that does not respond to nitrates.

indications.adult_dose: 25–100 mcg slow IV push/IM/IN/IO; consider half or reduced dose for patients >65 y/o; may repeat same dose for incomplete response after 10 min
indications.peds_dose: 2 mcg/kg slow IV push/IM/IN/IO (Max: 50 mcg); IN is preferred first-line route for severe pain in <14 y/o; may repeat for incomplete response
contraindications: Signs or symptoms of circulatory shock
SBP <100 mmHg
Signs of respiratory depression
Altered level of consciousness, mental status change, or suspected head injury
indications.when_to_give: For moderate-to-severe acute pain not adequately controlled by acetaminophen alone. Titrated to pain relief; repeated if response is incomplete after 10 minutes.

indications.adult_dose: 1.0 mg IM/IN (for patients ≥5 y/o)
indications.peds_dose: 0.5 mg IM/IN (for patients 1 month – 4 y/o)
indications.when_to_give: Alternative to IV dextrose when IV/IO access is unavailable in a hypoglycemic patient with AMS. Stimulates hepatic glycogenolysis to raise blood glucose.

indications.adult_dose: 1 mg IV/IO/IM
indications.peds_dose: 0.5 mg IV/IO/IM
indications.when_to_give: For beta blocker overdose with hemodynamic instability. Glucagon bypasses the blocked beta receptor to increase heart rate and contractility.

indications.adult_dose: 1 tube (15 g) PO
indications.peds_dose: ¼–½ tube PO
contraindications: Patient unable to protect airway or actively vomiting
indications.when_to_give: For alert, conscious hypoglycemic patients who are able to swallow and protect their airway. Applied inside the cheek if patient cannot swallow but is alert.

indications.adult_dose: N/A
indications.peds_dose: 5 mL applied inside cheek (massaged in)
contraindications: Do NOT delay or compromise airway placement or ventilation
indications.when_to_give: For newborn with POC glucose <50 g/dL AND IV/IO access cannot be established. Applied as a temporizing measure only — does not replace IV dextrose.

indications.adult_dose: 0.5 mg combined with Albuterol 2.5 mg, Nebulized; may repeat twice as needed
indications.peds_dose: 0.5 mg combined with Albuterol 2.5 mg, Nebulized; may repeat twice as needed
indications.when_to_give: Add-on to Albuterol for allergic reactions WITHOUT anaphylactic features (no hypotension, no severe signs, not ≥2 body systems) when wheezing/dyspnea is present. Given as combined unit dose with Albuterol 2.5 mg. Not used for anaphylaxis.

indications.adult_dose: Added to Albuterol 5 mg, Nebulized; no separate listed dose
indications.peds_dose: Added to Albuterol 5 mg, Nebulized; no separate listed dose
indications.when_to_give: Optional add-on to Albuterol for additional bronchodilation in asthma/COPD or reactive airway disease. No specific clinical threshold defined — per provider discretion.

indications.adult_dose: Combined with Albuterol 5 mg, Nebulized
indications.peds_dose: Combined with Albuterol 5 mg, Nebulized
indications.when_to_give: Optional add-on to Albuterol for bronchospasm from respiratory irritant chemical exposure.

indications.adult_dose: 40 mg; infuse over 30 sec, dwell 30 sec before flushing/use
indications.peds_dose: 0.5 mg/kg (Max: 40 mg); infuse over 30 sec, dwell 30 sec
indications.when_to_give: OPTIONAL — for conscious patients receiving an IO, when time permits. Given after the initial saline flush that clears the needle, before medication delivery.

indications.adult_dose: 5 g in 100–250 mL isotonic fluid IV/IO over 15 min for all suspected pre-eclampsia; if still seizing at 15 min, ADDITIONAL 3 g in 100–250 mL isotonic fluid IV/IO
indications.peds_dose: N/A (protocol applies to pregnant patients only)
indications.route: IV/IO (infusion in isotonic fluid over 15 min)
indications.when_to_give: For suspected pre-eclampsia (SBP >160 mmHg, DBP >110 mmHg, and/or symptomatic) and for seizures of eclampsia. Given to all suspected cases regardless of seizure activity; additional dose for refractory seizures.

indications.adult_dose: 2 g in 100–250 mL isotonic fluid IV/IO over 15 min
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Pediatric)
indications.when_to_give: For severe or worsening asthma/COPD after bronchodilators and NIPPV have been initiated. Provides bronchodilation through smooth muscle relaxation.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Respiratory Distress (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 40 mg/kg in 100–250 mL isotonic fluid IV/IO over 15 min (Max: 2 g)
indications.when_to_give: For severe or worsening pediatric asthma after bronchodilators have been given. Dose is 2 mL/kg if diluted in 100 mL, or 5 mL/kg if diluted in 250 mL, to achieve 40 mg/kg.

indications.adult_dose: 2 g IV/IO over 10 min
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Tachycardia – Unstable (Pediatric)
indications.when_to_give: For unstable wide-complex tachycardia confirmed as Torsades de Pointes (polymorphic VT with oscillating QRS amplitude). Given alongside synchronized cardioversion.

indications.adult_dose: N/A
indications.peds_dose: 40 mg/kg IV/IO over 10 min (Max: 2 g)
indications.when_to_give: For pediatric unstable Torsades de Pointes. Given alongside synchronized cardioversion.

indications.adult_dose: 5 mg IM or IV (if previously established); may repeat ONCE
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mg/kg IM/IN or IV (Max: 5 mg); may repeat ONCE
indications.route: IM (preferred for rapid delivery), IN, or IV
indications.when_to_give: First-line chemical restraint for acute behavioral disturbance when patient is a danger to themselves or others. May be repeated once if initial dose is insufficient.

indications.adult_dose: 2.5 mg IV/IM/IN; may repeat after 5–10 min
indications.peds_dose: Not specified in Chest Pain CPG
indications.when_to_give: For cocaine- or methamphetamine-related chest pain where sympathetic hyperactivation is the underlying mechanism. Benzodiazepine sedation reduces catecholamine-driven demand on the heart.

indications.adult_dose: 2.5 mg IV/IO OR 5 mg IN
indications.peds_dose: 0.05 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 2.5 mg) OR 0.1 mg/kg IM/IN (Max: 5 mg)
indications.route: IV/IO or IN (adult); IV/IO, IM, or IN (peds)
indications.when_to_give: For excessive shivering during active cooling in heat stroke. Shivering generates heat and counteracts cooling efforts; midazolam is used to suppress it.

indications.adult_dose: 10 mg IM or 5 mg IV/IO for active seizure; repeat same dose if still seizing after 5–10 min
indications.peds_dose: N/A
indications.when_to_give: For management of active seizures in eclampsia. Higher initial IM dose (10 mg) allows rapid delivery without IV access. Magnesium sulfate should also be given simultaneously for all pre-eclampsia/eclampsia cases.

indications.adult_dose: 2.5 mg IV/IO OR 5 mg IN/IM; may repeat ONCE after 5–10 min
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IN/IM (Max: 5 mg); may repeat ONCE
contraindications: Hemodynamically unstable patients — do not delay cardioversion or pacing for sedation
Apneic or unresponsive patients do not require sedation
Patients with respiratory distress from a concurrent condition
indications.when_to_give: For procedural sedation prior to transcutaneous pacing or direct current cardioversion. Also for managing post-ROSC agitation. Select midazolam OR ketamine — do not combine.

indications.adult_dose: 10 mg IM OR 5 mg IV/IO
indications.peds_dose: 0.2 mg/kg IN/IM OR 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO (Max: 1 mg)
indications.route: IM or IN (preferred — do NOT delay for IV/IO access); IV/IO also acceptable
contraindications: If signs of pregnancy, refer to OB/GYN – Pregnancy Complications CPG for eclampsia management (magnesium sulfate becomes primary)
indications.when_to_give: For any actively seizing patient. IM/IN route preferred to avoid delay. May repeat ONCE with same age-based dose if still seizing after 5 min. If still seizing after second dose, contact BioTel medical control.

indications.adult_dose: 0.4–0.5 mg IV/IO OR 2 mg IN; may repeat after 3 min
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM/IN (Max: 0.5 mg IV/IO/IM or 2 mg IN); may repeat after 3 min
indications.when_to_give: For respiratory depression (not just altered mental status alone) in a patient with suspected opioid toxicity. Titrate to adequate respiratory effort, not to full awakening.

indications.adult_dose: 0.4–0.5 mg IV/IO/IM OR 2 mg IN; repeat every 3 min for incomplete response
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mg/kg IV/IO/IM/IN (Max: 0.4 mg IV/IO/IM or 2 mg IN); repeat every 3 min
indications.when_to_give: For opioid-induced respiratory depression or apnea from overdose. Goal is restoration of adequate ventilation. Repeated as needed given opioid's duration may exceed naloxone's.

indications.adult_dose: 0.4 mg SL; may repeat every 3–5 min (up to 3 doses) if SBP >100 mmHg and HR >50 bpm
indications.peds_dose: Not listed (Chest Pain CPG is ≥14 y/o)
contraindications: SBP <100 mmHg
HR <50 bpm
Use of PDE5 inhibitor within 48 hours (sildenafil/Viagra, vardenafil/Levitra, tadalafil/Cialis)
indications.when_to_give: Given to chest pain patients after aspirin, if SBP is adequate and HR is above 50. Used for ischemic chest pain and CHF with adequate BP. Repeat up to 3 doses if pain persists and BP/HR remain within limits.

indications.adult_dose: 0.4 mg SL; every 5 min, up to 3 doses if SBP ≥100 mmHg
indications.peds_dose: N/A
contraindications: SBP <100 mmHg
HR <50 bpm
PDE5 inhibitor use within 48 hr
Do NOT use if patient is in shock
indications.when_to_give: For adult CHF/pulmonary edema with adequate SBP (≥100 mmHg) and mild-to-moderate respiratory distress. Reduces preload to relieve pulmonary congestion. Do not use if hypotensive.

indications.adult_dose: Infusion: 4–10 mcg/min IV/IO; mix 2 mg NE in 250 mL NS (NOT LR) = 8 mcg/mL
indications.peds_dose: Infusion: 0.1 mcg/kg/min IV/IO (Max: 10 mcg/min); mix 2 mg NE in 250 mL NS = 8 mcg/mL
contraindications: Do NOT mix with Lactated Ringer's
indications.when_to_give: For SBP <90 mmHg post-ROSC that does not respond to fluid resuscitation. Primary vasopressor for post-cardiac arrest hypotension.

indications.adult_dose: 4 mcg/min IV/IO infusion (Max: 10 mcg/min); titrate every 2–5 min to SBP ≥90 mmHg; mix 4 mg NE in 500 mL NS = 8 mcg/mL
indications.peds_dose: N/A — see Shock/Hypotension (Pediatric)
indications.route: IV/IO (continuous infusion); IV pump preferred if available
contraindications: Do NOT mix with Lactated Ringer's
indications.when_to_give: For cardiogenic or general medical shock not responding to isotonic fluid resuscitation. Titrated upward every 2–5 min until hypotension resolves.

indications.adult_dose: N/A — see Shock/Hypotension (Adult)
indications.peds_dose: 0.1 mcg/kg/min IV/IO infusion (Max: 10 mcg/min); titrate every 2–5 min; mix 4 mg NE in 500 mL NS = 8 mcg/mL
contraindications: Do NOT mix with Lactated Ringer's
Contact BioTel medical control if insufficient response
indications.when_to_give: For pediatric shock not responding to fluid resuscitation. Second-line after fluids; push dose epinephrine may be used first as a bridge.

indications.adult_dose: 4–8 mg PO/IV/IM
indications.peds_dose: Not approved
contraindications: QTc >500 ms on ECG
Not for patients <2 y/o
indications.when_to_give: For nausea and vomiting in patients ≥2 y/o, especially when vomiting is impairing patient care, assessment, or medication administration. Screen ECG when available.

indications.adult_dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO (Max: 50 mEq)
indications.peds_dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO (Max: 50 mEq)
indications.when_to_give: In the event of sudden cardiac arrest in a behavioral emergency patient, acidosis is considered a likely contributing cause. Sodium bicarbonate is given to buffer metabolic acidosis.

indications.indication: Drug Overdose / Drug Intoxication (Tricyclic Antidepressant Overdose)
indications.adult_dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO (Max: 50 mEq)
indications.peds_dose: 1 mEq/kg IV/IO (Max: 50 mEq)
indications.when_to_give: For widened QRS complex from tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) overdose. Sodium bicarbonate alkalinizes the blood and reverses sodium channel blockade, narrowing the QRS and reducing arrhythmia risk.
